title: Tests share the production ./books.db and assume it starts empty
evidence: bookstore_test.go:10 NewBookStore() opens ./books.db; the file is only removed in a trailing defer (bookstore_test.go:18,96,135), never before the test. TestGetAllBooks asserts len(books)==3 (bookstore_test.go:171), which fails if a books.db with residual rows already exists in the workspace.
suggestion: Use a unique temp DB per test (t.TempDir()) or an in-memory DSN (file::memory:?cache=shared), and clean before as well as after.

title: HTTP handler layer is not exercised by any test
evidence: bookstore_test.go imports only os/testing and calls BookStore methods directly; no net/http/httptest usage. main.go:228-318 handlers, status-code mapping (201/400/404/204/503) and JSON encoding are therefore unverified, and the string-matching error translation at main.go:237,265,288 is untested.
suggestion: Add httptest.NewRequest/ResponseRecorder tests covering create (201), missing-title (400), get-missing (404), delete (204), and /health.

title: GET /books returns JSON null instead of [] for an empty collection
evidence: main.go:79 declares `var books []Book` (nil slice); when no rows match it is encoded as `null` at main.go:259, not `[]`.
suggestion: Initialize `books := []Book{}` so an empty result serializes as an empty array.

title: CreateBook errors ignored in test setup
evidence: bookstore_test.go:161-163 call store.CreateBook without checking the returned error, so a failed insert would silently produce a misleading count assertion.
suggestion: Check and t.Fatal on setup CreateBook errors.

title: HTTP status mapping relies on substring matching of error strings
evidence: main.go:237,265,288,309 use strings.Contains(err.Error(), "not found"/"required") to choose status codes; brittle if error wording changes.
suggestion: Return typed/sentinel errors (errors.Is) from the store layer and map those to status codes.
